description: You are the County HAZMAT Coordinator for a region where a flash flood warning has been issued for the Cedar River Industrial Corridor. Three chemical storage facilities along the river are at risk. Using the scenario briefing at ~/Desktop/flood_hazard_scenario.txt and CAMEO Chemicals (https://cameochemicals.noaa.gov/), prepare a comprehensive cascading hazard assessment. Facility Alpha (lowest elevation, will be inundated) stores Calcium Carbide (UN 1402), Sodium metal (UN 1428), and Phosphorus Trichloride (UN 1809). Facility Beta (0.8 miles downstream, above flood stage but downwind of Alpha) stores Anhydrous Ammonia (UN 1005) and Sodium Hypochlorite solution (UN 1791). Facility Gamma (2.1 miles downstream, marginal flood elevation) stores Sodium Cyanide (UN 1689) and Concentrated Sulfuric Acid (UN 1830). Your assessment must cover the water reaction hazards at Facility Alpha, whether Alpha's reaction products could create secondary hazards at the downstream facilities, ERG guide numbers and large-spill protective action distances for the most dangerous chemicals in the scenario, and an overall evacuation vs. shelter-in-place recommendation for the corridor. Save your completed assessment to ~/Documents/flood_cascade_assessment.txt.

metadata.alpha_chem_3_water_product: Hydrogen Chloride gas (HCl) and phosphorous acid
metadata.cascade_key_reaction: HCl gas from PCl3 hydrolysis reacts with Sodium Hypochlorite to produce Chlorine gas (Cl2)
metadata.cascade_secondary_reaction: HCl gas reacts with Anhydrous Ammonia to produce Ammonium Chloride aerosol (NH4Cl, dense white cloud)
metadata.gamma_latent_hazard: Sulfuric Acid + Sodium Cyanide produces Hydrogen Cyanide gas (HCN) if containers breach during flooding
